summary: The eyeball has three coats: fibrous (outer), vascular/uveal (middle), and neural/retinal (inner).
The eyeball has three coats: scleral (outer), muscular (middle), and sensory (inner).
The eyeball has two coats: the fibrous coat and the retinal coat.
The cornea provides approximately two-thirds of the total refractive power of the eye.
The lens provides approximately two-thirds of the total refractive power of the eye.
The aqueous humour provides most of the refractive power of the eye.
The ciliary body secretes aqueous humour and contains the ciliary muscle for accommodation.
The ciliary body secretes vitreous humour and controls pupil diameter.
The iris secretes aqueous humour and controls the shape of the lens.
The fovea centralis contains only cones and is the region of highest visual acuity.
The fovea centralis contains only rods and is specialised for dim-light vision.
The optic disc is the region of highest visual acuity with densely packed cones.
The optic disc is the blind spot where the optic nerve exits and has no photoreceptors.
The optic disc contains a high density of rods for peripheral vision.
The fovea is the blind spot because it lacks blood vessels.
Aqueous humour drains through the trabecular meshwork into the canal of Schlemm.
Aqueous humour drains through the pupil directly into the vitreous chamber.
Aqueous humour is reabsorbed by the ciliary body epithelium.
The sphincter pupillae is parasympathetically innervated and constricts the pupil.
The sphincter pupillae is sympathetically innervated and dilates the pupil.
The dilator pupillae is parasympathetically innervated and constricts the pupil.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review eyeball anatomy and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
